# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud monitoring and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: my-app
  type: gcp-types/compute-v1:instances
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: n1-standard-1
    disks:
    - autoDelete: true
      boot: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/gce-uefi-images/global/images/ubuntu-2004-lts
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
    metadata:
      items:
      - key: startup-script
        value: |
          #!/bin/bash
          apt update && apt install -y nginx
          systemctl enable --now nginx
          echo "Hello, World!" > /var/www/html/index.html

- name: my-app-lb
  type: gcp-types/compute-v1:targetPools
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    instances: [my-app]
